objective: Final pre-build QA review of metaswarm and SwarmMesh readiness using the synced launcher and fixed Agent Pulse.
blockers: Task tool in this environment did not expose background agent ids; launch_started ledger entries use stable surrogate agent_id values sync::<worker_id>.
consensus_notes: Metaswarm opt-in and no-silent-downgrade policy are documented consistently in launcher prompts, commander agent instructions, README, and SKILL.md.
Main implementation gap is enforcement: banned-model/runtime model verification, depth guard, and partial-status validation rely heavily on prompts/contracts rather than hard launcher checks.
Documentation is not fully aligned on worker branch naming and operational teardown/recovery guidance.
conflict_notes: SKILL.md presents premium model handling as explicit user-selectable phrasing, while launcher metaswarm mode force-defaults a premium rotation and ordinary launcher default remains claude-haiku-4.5.
Commander contract defines success by launch counts; worker audits highlight that child completion/failure is not part of the success gate.

main_findings.summary: Launcher enforces a banned-model preflight for metaswarm arguments, but runtime child model compliance is not verified.
Depth Guard and partial/failure handling are mostly contractual/prompt-based rather than code-enforced.
Docs disagree on branch naming and differ in teardown/recovery detail.
Launcher seeds launch-proof telemetry but does not validate proof counts before success on the happy path.

findings: Launcher does not fully hard-enforce premium/banned model policy outside metaswarm gating; default MODEL is claude-haiku-4.5 and --agent-cmd can bypass model templating.
Metaswarm max-5 commanders error message claims total cap 1600 even though 5 x 250 implies 1250 leaf workers.
Teardown/recovery UX is inconsistent: usage suggests --teardown --run-id alone, but repo-local cleanup depends on repo context.
Launch-proof files are seeded and prompted, but launcher success path does not verify proof counts before declaring completion.
Commander agent contract does not require an explicit launcher-side metaswarm-mode verification before task claim and child spawning.
Success criteria gate only on launch counts, not downstream child completion/failure outcomes.
Premium/banned model policy requires explicit premium selection but does not require runtime verification that launches honored requested models.
Stop/teardown/recovery instructions are underspecified for already-launched children and interrupted commander state.
SKILL.md correctly makes metaswarm explicit opt-in and requires partial/failed rather than silent downgrade for incomplete full-child launch.
SKILL.md lacks banned-model validation in parse/launch phrasing, so a user-supplied banned model string is not blocked at the skill layer.
Depth Guard is implicit through squad allocation/leaf instructions rather than enforced as an explicit numeric depth check.
Recovery is well-defined, but there is no explicit graceful stop command beyond teardown.
README and AGENTS disagree on worker branch naming: README uses stampede/task-001 style while AGENTS documents stampede/<agent-id>/<task-id>.
README documents runtime proof files and premium child-model defaults for metaswarm more explicitly than AGENTS.
Neither README nor AGENTS documents banned-model enforcement in depth.
README documents teardown, while AGENTS lacks parallel stop/teardown/recovery guidance.
Banned models are rejected at metaswarm launch time, but runtime verification of actual child model use is not enforced beyond telemetry logging.
Depth Guard is documented/prompted rather than implemented as code-level enforcement in stampede.sh.
Partial status is instructed in commander prompts, but launcher code does not validate bundle status against actual worker launch counts.
Smoke test checks banned_child_models metadata presence but does not execute a negative banned-model rejection test.
